records.ety: [F. rsulter, fr. L. resultare, resultarum, to spring or leap back, v. intens. fr. resilire. See Resile.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Resulted; p. pr. & vb. n. Resulting.]

quotes: The huge round stone, resulting with a bound.

defs: To come out, or have an issue; to terminate; to have consequences; -- followed by in; as, this measure will result in good or in evil.
To proceed, spring, or rise, as a consequence, from facts, arguments, premises, combination of circumstances, consultation, thought, or endeavor.
quotes: Pleasure and peace do naturally result from a holy and good life.
Sound is produced between the string and the air by the return or the result of the string.

defs: That which results; the conclusion or end to which any course or condition of things leads, or which is obtained by any process or operation; consequence or effect; as, the result of a course of action; the result of a mathematical operation.
quotes: If our proposals once again were heard, We should compel them to a quick result.

defs: The decision or determination of a council or deliberative assembly; a resolve; a decree.
quotes: Then of their session ended they bid cry With trumpet's regal sound the great result.
